title: [FIRRTL] Zero Width Bug in Shift/Div Folders
body:
b26bf21a96b29d69f742eff1ed4b2b2420453a6b introduced a bug in folding related to shift (and probably div) of zero width things. This is also the reason that `circt/perf` is failing it's test1 regression. The following test case shows the problem:

```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input a: UInt<0>
    output b: UInt<1>

    b <= dshr(a, UInt<1>(1))
```

Currently this crashes with:

```
Assertion failed: (width && "Can't truncate to 0 bits"), function trunc, file /Users/schuylere/repos/github.com/llvm/circt/llvm/llvm/lib/Support/APInt.cpp, line 860.
```

The problem originates from this line:

```
frame #6: 0x000000010077761a firtool`constFoldFIRRTLBinaryOp(op=0x00000001098be1b0, operands=ArrayRef<mlir::Attribute> @ 0x000070000b904e08, opKind=DivideOrShift, calculate=0x000070000b904e48)> const&) at FIRRTLFolds.cpp:175:31
   172 	  // If the result type is smaller than the computation then we need to
   173 	  // narrow the constant after the calculation.
   174 	  if (opKind == BinOpKind::DivideOrShift)
-> 175 	    resultValue = resultValue.truncOrSelf(resultType.getWidthOrSentinel());
   176 	
   177 	  return getIntAttr(resultType, resultValue);
   178 	}
```

On the Scala FIRRTL Compiler, this goes through to produce:

```verilog
module Foo(
  output  b
);
  assign b = 1'h0;
endmodule
```
